title: Combined Robot Trial
description: Our first end-to-end trial combining both robots in a single sequence. The mobile robot was calibrated to drive in a straight line by tuning the individual motor speeds — small differences in motor characteristics caused it to veer, so we adjusted PWM values until the path was consistent. We then used the ultrasonic sensor to program a precise stop distance, bringing the robot to a halt a fixed distance from a target object. With the mobile robot in position, the robot arm took over: we manually guided it through a series of poses, saved each one, and then triggered a replay sequence — allowing the arm to autonomously reach out and touch the mobile robot as the final step. The trial demonstrated both robots working as a coordinated system for the first time.
